author: @jonazo7188
text: The irony of every female character becoming a girlboss is that they inadvertently sidelined their OG girlboss who carried the show for so many seasons: the one and only Eleven. The best emotional storyline of the show is Eleven, a buzzcut androgynous test subject feral child, finally getting the chance to be treated like a daughter and a girlfriend, i.e., a GIRL. When she puts on a dress for the first time and Mike sees her and goes gaga over her. Hopper setting boundaries and being protective and loving like the father she never had. And the best parts of the finale are the moments that touch on this storyline, but only to wreck it in the cruelest way possible. Hopper and Mike have to watch the one girl they loved the most disappear from their lives forever (whether or not she truly dies). It’s a rather depressing ending. The big question of the show, as it related to Eleven, who was always the central character, is “will she one day live a normal life, if she is shown enough love?” And it’s answered with a resounding NO.

author: @salamander5318
text: There was a really funny easter egg inside joke from VFX artists on the show when Henry was about to enter the cave and Will was seeing what Henry was seeing through the hive mind. There was a BIG difference about what Will saw though. An image of The mind flayer is superimposed with the cave entrance in Will's mind at that point. The mind flayer looks EXACTLY like a Vulva. Entering the cave is Henry's ultimate fear and the VFX Artists showed the Vuvla as WILL's ULTIMATE FEAR.

replies.author: @DavidCraig-er6kq
replies.text: That's my thoughts too. At the end of the day they will get the viewing hours they need from the current fan base for watching season 5. Most people are too committed not to watch it. And it's the last season so it doesn't matter. And the spin offs are much lower budget so less risk of a loss due to the fallout of a piss poor final season. Goes all the way back to the series 'Lost'. They have been pulling that trick of creating mystery and intrigue to keep you entrenched in the show with no clue how to pull it together at the end, and firing out any old bs because their ideas won't tie together because they never started with the end in mind. They could have finished the show off perfectly by adding another 3 or 4 episodes at most to season 4, and working within what they had already created. Lazy script writing and corporate greed to get another full season out of it.

author: @Mattblaze1477
text: I remember a few years ago hearing that story about the kid from Stranger Things crying because his lines at the comic-cons were shorter than his cast-mates. I havent watched an episode of the show since I saw that headline. Just one small sniff of that social justice nonsense and I will drop the product without even a second thought. Another thing, the entire cast of Children aged right off a cliff.......(I'm just keeping it real here so those with delicate sensibilities should stop reading)........The young boys grew up and now have zero on screen appeal. The cute little kids schtick is gone and leftover we get weird looking gangly teens I can barely look at. Stranger Things is one of the more overrated television shows of all time. They created 1 season of brilliance and the rest of the show lived in season 1's shadow for the remainder of its run. All the money spent on this slop should have gone to Mindhunter instead.

I absolutely fell in love with that movie when it came out. I wish more people talked about it. My friends didnt like it. Watched it again recently back in like February after buying the 4K steelbook. Still fuckin great.

Over the years I've probably seen the movie 10+ times. Such a breezy watch. Back when Hollywood still took risks. 2011 is also the same year Real Steel came out another scifi movie. Maybe not full on scifi but it does have scifi elements. In Time another underrated film that year but not as good as Real Steel or Super 8. The Adventures of Tintin another underrated gem. All 3 are either produced by Spielberg or directed by him. 2011 was such a huge year for him. 2 directorial efforts and 3 or 4 producing credits

replies.author: @tman231
replies.text:
Things went just fine, but Jeremy calls everything a disaster because that’s what gets him views

How about watching the show for yourself and forming your own opinions instead of letting some random misogynist loser make them for you

replies.author: @thefantasticretroreviewer3941
replies.text: ​@tman231Fine

replies.author: @tman231
replies.text:
@thefantasticretroreviewer3941 Sorry. I’m not trying to be rude. I don’t know anything about you or how familiar you are with Geeks + Gamers. 

Geeks + Gamers is a really horrible community filled with a lot of bad people, Jeremy being towards the top of that list, and it bothers me to see people take their opinions seriously.

I’m not saying that you’re not allowed to criticize things. Constructive criticism is welcome. I myself have plenty of issues with Stranger Things 5. These guys always have the same issues though: “the message” or “wokeness”.

There’s a huge difference between actually having issues related to the quality of the content and just complaining every time a non-straight white male does anything

replies.author: @robertharper3114
replies.text: ​@tman231Seems personal for you! 😂

replies.author: @tman231
replies.text:
@robertharper3114 And? It is personal. I’m passionate about movies and TV, and people like Jeremy are a horrible influence on that community.

Even beyond movies and TV, I would think that anyone with any sense of right and wrong could watch this video and tell that Jeremy is in the wrong. Most of the video is literally just him complaining about the women and LGBTQ+ characters in Stranger Things.

This is coming from someone who is a straight white male. When watching Stranger Things 5, the thought of “Linda Hamilton’s character should’ve been played by a man” never entered my mind. The thought of Nancy being “too bad*ss” for a woman never centered my mind. I was never keeping track of the number of gay characters.

That’s the twisted lens that Jeremy and the rest of his crew view the world through though and, whether they know it or not, they’re training their audience to think the same way.

So yes: It is personal, I do care, and there’s nothing I’d love to see more than for people like Jeremy to get their due. They’re the epitome of what’s wrong with fandom and the internet. They just like to act like they’re the good guys

replies.author: @robertharper3114
replies.text: ​@tman231You act like these grievances haven't been building up for the last 10 years! Just because you are able to ignore the blatant feminist leftism doesn't mean others can. In my opinion, the reason you are so upset with Jeremy is because you know he has valid complaints. 🤷‍♂️

author: @DrBeef216
text: This show had amazing character arcs. Their agenda was kept at bay enough in prior seasons that I didn't care about the sprinkling in. A lot of heart. It had been one of my all-time favorite shows. And they completely ruined it for me, even for future rewatches, because it leads to nothing, same as GoT or Dexter.  The bottom line is that they only cared about their progressive agenda, crapped on Christianity with their blasphemous cussing, and completely didn't give a f*** about even decent writing or continuity. Some of the absolute worst writing I've ever seen. Even the Duffers going, paraphrasing, "the demogorgons - yeah, they're around." What a disgrace.

author: @thebrowneyesofmandalore
text: It’s ridiculous how little meaning the Upside Down had at this point. The whole military threat and plot line at this point was so meaningless, overblown, and overused, that it came at the expense of the threat of the Upside Down. The threat the main characters should’ve been facing while dealing with Vecna should have been an extension of him, as it had been, for continuity, that being the monsters in the Upside Down. Not the tiresome military arc.

author: @BrolyPowerMaximum
text:
I agree. 10:47 the Wheeler Mom couldn’t be allowed to look bad as a woman.  Therefore, she suddenly has the power to melee tank a gorgon for 30 seconds when bullets from three soldiers do absolutely nothing to them.  I’m surprised they even added the Wheeler father back into the story in the final episode just to take up space, seems like all his job was was to be useless in comparison.

Then she silently puts a gas tank in a cloth washer in the most ridiculous scene to finally distracted the Gorgons with sound where she was somehow making none, and they couldn’t find Lucas with his boom box booming in a silly Jurassic Park reference.

Then there is Holly, who we are told it doesn’t need to no Lucas man to save her because she is so strong and so brave, she can just make her own portal, pity max, and she didn’t have that conversation before they got to the portal for Max.

The army is completely incompetent throughout the final season and really doesn’t accomplish any purpose in the story like for being the plot device that makes 11 make a choice.  Nancy is more effective than the entire army, as as shown when she just nonchalantly, takes out five people at a time without any future consequence.

Speaking of Nancy again, she’s so independent, unlike the other adults and unlike the other characters and she don’t need no Jonathan Man - so she’s going to let him know that and how  she wants to focus on her career and explore other men even as they are literally dying in a cosmic melting room of goop.  By the way, I don’t like Jonathan, either.  But it’s just such a tired talking point and idea with women.

I was just talking with my female cousin who is 44 at another female cousin’s funeral the other day and she kept on telling me how regretful she was that she was 44 without children and it was hard to have a conversation with her because I made different choices in my life with my life and I have three boys.  It’s so tired.  What these cultural messages have done not just to men, but what they have done to women is disgusting.

Back to the plot holes though. Holly just happens to turn over a rock that makes her fall directly on top of the lab through the worm hole?  And what was the point of Holly even escaping as much as she did, if Vecna was able to just pull her up from an even greater distance than when he and she were next to the mindflayer?  Why didn’t he just grab her then, so that we could have an explanation of how a wormholes work?  So we could see how brave Holly was?  It was entirely pointless.

Also, where were any of the gorgans or bats or anything else in the upside down or in the other dimension X/abyss end of the wormhole?  There have been hundreds of these things running or flying around in Hawkins and suddenly they have no foot soldiers?  Convenient.

Also, what was up with the choice to violate the children with softcore tentacle scenes?  If the mind flayer particles are going to now simply exhale as a gas, when the things come, unconnected, which breaks the plot of everything they had to do to will to get the particles out of him earlier on, why can’t the gas just slow slowly be inhaled, instead of some forced tentacle action on little children? It’s really a disgusting choice in my opinion.

But isn’t that ironic, the people who claim the moral superiority of storytelling or so interested and invested in throwing tentacles down children’s throats and showing really disgusting and graphic scenes of material being pumped into them?

author: @fatmanpez
text: I liked Nancy and Robin's development, Holly and Derek's addition was good to though it would have been interesting for Erica to have been the younger kid to get absorbed by Henry rather than Holly but Erica would have walked back out the minute she entered that house with all the kids. The only overly virtue signaling thing they pushed this season was how long and pointless Will's coming out scene was because it wasted time that could have been used for more depth as to why he thought he had to (which was pointless because it's hardly ever acknowledged in the final episode anyway. . ..), Steve held everyone together and grew, Hopper still did Hopper things, the four boys all did a lot the only character who didn't do much this season was Johnathan. The biggest problem with this season is how many loose ends they left open that could have been resolved even with just a character saying something at some point or even a brief camera angle showing something like Vicki with a new partner, Johnathan calling Argoyle, Sarah Conner being relieved from her position for failing to capture 11, stuff like that could have made the episode at least feel complete.

author: @nunyabiz012
text:
Can we address the fact that NONE of Vecna's plan made any sense?  They already have the holes between Dimension X, the Upside Down, and Hawkins, right?  Why not send an army of DemoThings through, wipe out the military, establish a base, and THEN capture the children for the ultimate plan - which also makes no sense whatsoever.... 
Did that ginormous WALL which connects our world to dimension X just somehow grow with the lowering of Dimension X?  Wouldn't the same thing that happened to the Radio tower also happen to the wall, and de-stabilize it, and thus wipe out the upside down?  
The premise is interesting for sure.... but the writing is so bad in this last season......

author: @nihongo-shoyo10
text:
So in the end just another example of the inability to create entertaining TV shows, movies, or anything related to entertainment and to ruin it with a modern message. 
You could understand it in 1, 2, or 3 out of 7 TV shows or movies, but Western entertainment has this annoying habit of presenting it in all 50 TV shows or movies. It's tiresome. 
There are more interesting films and tv shows from Asia or Europe that don't focus on this alphabetical nonsense.
Keep going Hollywood, so more sooner than later audience will losing interest and studios will be closing for good!😁

author: @andyreaver1962
text: So all of the sudden the military and Hawkins lab loose interest in the entire town of Hawkins and we never see the Fallout of what happens with Kay? Where is Dr Owens? How did Max Graduate? Max has been in a coma for like a year. What was the government cover up story for season 5?! Is no one gonna check on Derrick’s parents? At least Steve got a decent ending. Nancy doesn’t deserve him. Why the hell did they dress Nancy like Hillary Clinton in the end!? lol she looked awful. I have to agree Nancy’s character this season felt so off. Especially if you compare her to season 1 Nancy.

author: @Greenelation
text: I was just disappointed. I though season 4 was a gem. It brought it back for me and i was invested. Season 5, the fact they started on a calm mood after season 4 ended on hawkins split in 4 - should have kept that tone and hit ground running. The army in all of season 5 was just a plot point to have the demodogs kill someone but spare all the main characters. No one died. Where was suzie, elevens mum, so much was waisted this season dialog and waisted episodes. The killing of military personnel and the fact that there were no consequences for hopper, nancy? Im just disappointed

author: @arnoldtettero8470
text:
Yeah i can agree with the fact that the show as a whole is a 7 out of 10. This season however was bad as I can get that Robin may think nobody knew Will was gay. 

As she barely had interactions with him ,but most people in that room knew. Joyce and Hopper had a conversation in s1 where Joyce basically said her husband mocked and bullied will for he assumed he was gay. The bullies were mocking him by calling him a fairy.

S3 mike basically said it out loud when he ended that argument with that it not our fault your not into girls. His brother comforting him when he finally accepted Mike would never love him.

For me the only good part was the tease we got was that Dustin survived . The science teacher came back ,dipshit Derrick and having Joyce get a moment to kill vecna .

Now the fact that Dustin storyline where he tried to protect Eddie legacy and keep hellfire alive was dropped after the asskicking he got from the basketball team was stupid. How nobody died besides someone we don't care about. The army being useless. The fact all the ubkillable characters got wrecked or injured by almost nothing when the heroes face them ,but the army can't hurt them. You are telling me a damn glass bottle is more dangerous then a machine-gun

author: @maclnmacln
text: Vecna too stupid to anticipate a sneak attack. Vecna too stupid to fortify a major weak spot in his defenses. 11 and the Gang too stupid to beat Vecna, an entity with zero strategic sense. Took 5 years to beat Vecna. All the single season characters that died were obviously more stupid than Vecna and 11 and the Gang since they died. That's what the Duffer brothers did to the legacy of the characters by how the finale played out

replies.text: Nothing worth watching. They just keep doing the same show over and over again. That's the true problem of woke is it leads to creative bankruptcy. The question becomes, "why should I watch your varient when there is a market flooded with other options of the same thing?" The market is saturated with supply while customers have no demand for the product or service.
